frenchExemption.grade9: Students who have completed Grade 8 in their home country are not required to take French as a Second Language. An equivalent credit will be granted.

storefronts.mcv4uSequence: MCV4U will be taken after MHF4U is completed.
storefronts.taglineStack: Flexible Learning Pathways. Learn First. Decide Later. Succeed Safely.

upgradeAffordance.text: Want to earn Ontario credit for this course? Upgrade to the Official Ontario Program.

storefronts.grade8Transition: Grade 8 students: prepare for the Grade 9 academic bridge. Consider enrolling directly in the Official Ontario Program for Grade 9 to begin earning OSSD credits from day one, or continue with the Academic Preparation Program to validate readiness first.

gradeGroupPanel.leftDescription: The Academic Preparation Program builds strong foundations in Academic English, mathematics, science, social studies, and essential learning skills. Aligned with Ontario standards, it develops analytical thinking and disciplined study habits, preparing students for a smooth transition into OSSD credit courses or the Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD) program.
gradeGroupPanel.rightHeading: UPGRADE to Official Ontario Program (Ontario student record)
gradeGroupPanel.rightDescription: The Official Ontario Program is a fully accredited Canadian secondary education pathway designed for students pursuing academic excellence and global university opportunities. Aligned with Ontario curriculum standards, the program emphasizes strong academic foundations, critical thinking, and disciplined learning. With personalized academic guidance and a structured learning environment, students are prepared for university admission in Canada and internationally. Upon successful completion, students earn the Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD).
gradeGroupPanel.upgradeNote: Students who started in the Academic Preparation Program may upgrade at any point during the academic year. Previous coursework contributes to the Ontario student record upon upgrade.

bundleCard.includes: Includes all Math, Science, and English courses

coursePage.creditAssurance: This course awards 1.0 Ontario high school credit upon successful completion. Transcripts and report cards provided. Recognized by OUAC/OCAS and universities.
